steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Ellis found that social dimensions made users "feel more mindful of how their listening activities may be perceived," which then changed how they managed those activities. The right answer must show a user self-consciously adjusting listening behavior because of awareness of social observation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Listening to music through a streaming platform feels a lot more passive, since I don't need to make any deliberate choices about what music I'm going to consume, like I do when buying digital downloads or records and CDs.": Describes a user passively playing music without any mention of social awareness or behavior adjustment. ✗
  • Choice B — "When I create playlists or choose songs to listen to, I often pick songs that I think the friends who are following me might be interested in listening to, rather than what I might actually prefer listening to in that moment.": Shows the speaker choosing music based on what "friends who are following me might be interested in" rather than personal preference — exactly the perception-driven behavior shift Ellis documented. ✓
  • Choice C — "Because the predictions the platform makes about what new songs I might like depend on my past listening habits, at times its recommendations can feel overly repetitive and unvarying.": Focuses on the platform's algorithm, not the user's social self-awareness. ✗
  • Choice D — "The social aspects of music streaming make it really easy to create a sense of a shared listening experience with my friends, even if we aren't in the same room.": Discusses connecting with others generally but not the conscious modification of listening driven by how others perceive it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
